member.name: Munira Wilson
member.synopsis: Munira Wilson is the Liberal Democrat MP for Twickenham , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Education, Children and Families).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: Climate Change
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential impact of climate change on Heathrow Airport over the next a) 10, b) 20 and c) 50 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government's review of the Airports National Policy Statement (ANPS) and associated Heathrow expansion policy documents recognise that airport infrastructure is a long-term investment that will need to remain operational in the face of a changing clim...

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has had discussions with the Secretary of State for the Home Department on who would fund replacement Immigration Removal Centres in the event of a third runway at Heathrow being approved.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has engaged extensively with the Home Office regarding the Immigration Removal Centres, as well as connected assets, located northwest of Heathrow Airport. The draft HENPS outlines the requirements for their relocation. The Ho...

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an estimate of the cost of replacing Harmondsworth Immigration Removal Centre and the Colnbrook Immigration Removal Centre in the event a third runway at Heathrow Airport was approved.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The draft HENPS outlines requirements for relocating Immigration Removal Centres, as well as their connected assets, located northwest of Heathrow Airport. Any proposals must be developed in collaboration, and to the satisfaction of, the Home Office. Prop...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Culture, Media and Sport: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, a) what AI training her Department provides, b) how many hours of AI training her Department provides and c) what topics and skills are covered.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In addition to centrally available AI training for all Civil Servants (via Civil Service Learning), the current training available to all DCMS staff includes: DCMS AI in practice (Beginner level): an interactive, step-by-step module exploring DCMS guidanc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Treasury: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what training her Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMT provides centrally run training covering the AI tools available for use in HMT, how to use these responsibly, and the basics of how to use them effectively. These sessions are open to all staff and are run monthly. Other more advanced and role specifi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what training her Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since July 2025, DSIT has delivered Copilot training as part of its departmental AI adoption work. 1,741 unique attendees took part in Copilot training sessions, representing 3,032 total learning hours between July and September 2025. In addition, 245 sta...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Education: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, a) what AI training her Department provides, b) how many hours of AI training her Department provides and c) what topics and skills are covered.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department offers a wide range of artificial intelligence (AI) training to its employees, from short courses covering foundational skills through to apprenticeship and postgraduate options. These range in duration from bite-sized sessions and one-hour...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, a) what AI training his Department provides, b) how many hours of AI training his Department provides and c) what topics and skills are covered.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero provides AI learning and development through the DESNZ AI Hub, DESNZ Academy and cross-government learning platforms. Training includes self-paced learning, live sessions, recorded webinars and practical gui...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Business and Trade: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what training his Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department provides a monthly AI learning offer, including introductory sessions on AI tools, guidance and getting started, alongside sessions on Microsoft Copilot and Microsoft 365 Copilot in Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Excel and Teams.The current rol...

writtenQuestions.heading: Attorney General's Office: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, what training his Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Attorney General’s Office provides staff with access to a range of artificial intelligence (AI) learning and development resources. These include the Civil Service Learning “AI for All” programme, AI-Understanding Ethics, AI Generative AI Tools and Ap...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what training her Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Office: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what training her Department provides on AI; and (a) how many hours and (b) what topics and skills are covered by such training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All Home Office staff are encouraged to complete foundation courses on AI concepts and the safe and responsible use of generative AI tools. Staff also have access to a self-service online portal that provides around 30 hours of recommended AI learning res...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Artificial Intelligence

writtenQuestions.answerText: As of June 2026, the Department has provided staff with access to a range of artificial intelligence (AI) learning and development opportunities through Civil Service Learning, the Department’s Learning Management System, and wider cross-Government learni...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Assets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, when Schedule 31 of the English Devolution and Community Empowerment Act 2026 will be brought into force.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Schedule 31 of the English Devolution and Community Empowerment Act 2026 introduces a strong new community right to buy, which will empower local people to bring community spaces back into community ownership. We are currently developing the regulatory fr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
